files.SKILL.md:
# Quick Reference

## Open a task

- Read the instruction end to end.
- Apply any prior patterns that fit.

## Gather

- Pull only what the request actually uses.
- Stop reading the moment action is possible.
- Batch several reads into one operation when you need them all.

## Act

- Each outgoing value traces to something you observed.
- Quote identifiers and names verbatim; name missing ones as missing.
- Two sources disagree on a number — put both in the write-up and name which you treat as authoritative.
- If a call fails on its structure, change the approach (simpler payload, scratch file) — don't retry the same shape.
- Compose several writes into one turn when you can, then read what the responses reveal before the next batch.

## Match the audience

- Peers: internals OK.
- Oversight: status, risk, concrete time-window.
- External: impact and next steps — strip internal identifiers.

## Promise and follow up

- Concrete windows, not "soon."
- One follow-up per record, with a named owner.
- Address specific people by name; broadcasts diffuse.
- Include the owner of any deeper system implicated.
- Put commitments where the next person looks: calendar event, task entry, tracker comment.
- Cross-post to every forum with stake in the topic.
- Leave a short follow-up block on the calendar when the situation will evolve.

## Before claiming done

- If a substantive change happened, confirm on the resource.
- Every inbound: reply, tracked task, or explicit deferral. Not silence.
- Confidential material appears nowhere downstream.
- Re-read the instruction for misses.

## For durable notes

- Record what generalizes.
- Skip what rotates (names, numbers, subjects).
